description: Example labeled pairs for calibration / metrics. Extend with real human grades.

items.student_text: Plants use sunlight in chloroplasts to make sugar from CO2 and water and release oxygen.
items.reference_text: Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy stored in glucose using chloroplasts.

items.student_text: It is how plants eat light to grow.

items.student_text: Chlorophyll absorbs light; Calvin cycle fixes carbon into sugars in the stroma.

items.student_text: Light-dependent reactions produce ATP and NADPH; light-independent reactions build glucose.
